action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 12 failed immediately with a RuntimeError because the model weights were not moved to CUDA before the forward pass. The current best score is 0.8514 from iteration 9 (ConvNeXt-Tiny, 20 epochs natural CE + post-hoc logit adjustment). Analysis: To beat 0.8514, I will switch to the much stronger CLIP ViT-B/16 pretrained model (86M params, available offline via timm). It provides significantly richer visual representations than ConvNeXt-Tiny. I will train it with the same proven recipe as iteration 9 (natural sampling, standard CE, layer-wise LR decay, drop_path=0.1, RandAugment, 20 epochs), but using the model's native preprocessing mean/std extracted from timm's default_cfg so the backbone sees correctly normalized inputs. I will also fix the CUDA bug by explicitly calling model.to(device) and add gradient clipping for training stability. After training, I will tune tau with the updated tune_tau.py and run a smoke test before submitting eval. If CLIP ViT scores >0.85, I can ensemble it with the iteration 9 ConvNeXt in a follow-up iteration for even higher balanced accuracy.
action.command: python train.py --arch vit_clip --epochs 20 --drop_path 0.1 --lr 1e-3 --backbone_lr_decay 0.1 --output_name vit_clip.pt --seed 123 && python tune_tau.py --checkpoint checkpoints/vit_clip.pt --output checkpoints/vit_clip_adj.pt && cp /data/val.pt /data/test.pt && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/vit_clip_adj.pt --output /tmp/smoke.json && rm /data/test.pt && echo "Smoke test passed"
